characters.name: Ambrosia Malspar
characters.description: Ambrosia MalsparGeorgia Engel as Ambrosia MalsparBiographical informationGenderFemaleJob/Career:Meter MaidSpouse:ExidorBehind the scenesActorGeorgia EngelAppeared onMork and MindyEpisodes appeared in:2 in Season 2 Ambrosia Malsparis a character who appears in twoSeason 2episodes ofMork and Mindy, first"The Exidor Affair"(episode #14), then in"Exidor's Wedding"(episode #16). The part of Ambrosia is played in the episodes byGeorgia Engel. Ambrosia, a meter maid, who's the girlfriend (and later wife) ofExidor. It appears she left Exidor during season 3 after he said his "wife left him", but details were never given. It was likely she was unable to continue living with Exidor's mentality or she could no longer stand the presence of his annoying and intrusive imaginary friend Pepe.

characters.name: Chachi Arcola
characters.description: Chachi ArcolaScott Baio as ChachiBiographical informationAKA/Nickame(s):"Chachi" or "Chach"BirthnameCharles ArcolaJob/Career:Student at Jefferson High School from season 4, graduated in season 9Waiter/cook at Arnold's season 7-9Musician/drummer of Richie's bandLead singer/Musician in his own band in season 11Family:Louisa Fonzarelli Arcola Delvecchio(mother)Al Delvecchio(stepfather)Arthur Fonzarelli(cousin)Grandma Nussbaum(grandmother)Raymond "Spike" Fonzarelli(cousin)Angel Fonzarelli(cousin)Howard C. Cunningham(father-in-law)Marion Kelp Cunningham(mother-in-law)Chuck Cunningham(brother-in-law)Richie Cunningham(brother-in-law)Lori Beth Cunningham(sister-in-law)Richie Cunningham, Jr. (nephew-in-law)Roger Phillips(cousin-in-law)Leopold "Flip" Phillips(cousin-in-law)K.C. Cunningham(cousin-in-law)Spouse:Joanie CunninghamAbout:Favorite Food is Hamburgers and Cheeseburgers, soda and beerBig fan of Elvis Presley and The Beach BoysLoves playing and listening to rock n' roll musicLived with his mother in Seasons 4-9Became roommates with Potsie Webber in their apartment season 11Behind the scenesActorScott BaioAppeared onHappy Days/Joanie Loves ChachiEpisodes appeared in:130 onHappy Days, 22 onJoanie Loves Chachi Charles "Chachi" Arcolawas a recurring and later a main character on the ABC-TV seriesHappy Daysand then a main character on its' spinoff, ABC-TV'sJoanie Loves Chachi. Chachi was played byScott Baioin both series. Chachi is Fonzie's cousin.  Chachi looked up to Fonzie and wanted to be just like him, and like Fonzie he too is an excellent mechanic.  Chachi also proved to be an excellent musician and became the drummer of Richie Cunningham's band. Also similar to Fonzie, Chachi sees Howard and Marion Cunningham as his surrogate parents.

characters.name: Baba Gentle
characters.description: Baba GentleRichard Moll appears as cult leader Baba Gentle in the Season 4 episode "Ailenation" (ep.#9).Biographical informationAbout:Is orderd to "train"Mearth, whom was found by the cult wandering in the airport, after Mearth ran away from home after being told by Mork that he's an ailenGets convinced that Mork's divinely inspired when he makes a spark causing a fireplace to burnBehind the scenesActorRichard MollAppeared onMork and MindyEpisodes appeared in:"Alienation" in Season 4 Baba Gentlewas a character who appears in theSeason 4episode ofMork & Mindytitled"Alienation"(episode #9). The part of Baba Hope is played byRichard Moll, who would later become well known to TV viewers as Bull Shannon on the long-running NBC-TV seriesNight Court, withJohn Larroquette, who plays cult leaderBaba Hopein the episode. WhenMearth, who, in feeling like an outcast, and that he's "unloved" by his parents upon learning fromMorkthat he's an ailen, decides to run away from home, he winds up joining up with a cult called "The Utopians", which is led byBaba Hope(John Larroquette) as he's convinced that the cult really loves him, as Mork, posing as "Baba Loo", and Mindy both infiltrate the cult to try to retrive him. Baba Gentle, who seems rather honest in his beliefs in the cult, but somewhat dim-witted, is ordered by Baba Hope to "train" Mearth on the importance of ridding themselves of their worldly possessions.

characters.name: Baba Hope
characters.description: Baba HopeJohn Larroquette appears as cult leader Baba Hope in the Season 4 episode "Ailenation" (ep.#9).Biographical informationAbout:Refuses to releaseMearth, whom he found wandering in the airport, after Mearth ran away from home after being told by Mork that he's an ailenReleases Mearth when Mork makes a spark causing a fireplace to burnBehind the scenesActorJohn LarroquetteAppeared onMork and MindyEpisodes appeared in:"Alienation" in Season 4 Baba Hopewas a character who appears in theSeason 4episode ofMork & Mindytitled"Alienation"(episode #9). The part of Baba Hope is played byJohn Larroquette, who would later become well known to TV viewers as Dan Fielding on the long-running NBC-TV seriesNight Court, a role for which he won four Emmy Awards for during the show's run. WhenMearth, who, in feeling like an outcast, and that he's "unloved" by his parents upon learning fromMorkthat he's an ailen, decides to run away from home, he winds up joining up with a cult called "The Utopians", which is led by Baba Hope andBaba Gentle(played byRichard Moll), as he's convinced that the cult really loves him, as Mork, posing as "Baba Loo", and Mindy both infiltrate the cult to try to retrive him, as Baba Hope orders Baba Gentle, who acts as the cult's "enforcer", to seize them.

The part of Baba Hope is played byJohn Larroquette, who would later become well known to TV viewers as Dan Fielding on the long-running NBC-TV seriesNight Court, a role for which he won four Emmy Awards for during the show's run. WhenMearth, who, in feeling like an outcast, and that he's "unloved" by his parents upon learning fromMorkthat he's an ailen, decides to run away from home, he winds up joining up with a cult called "The Utopians", which is led by Baba Hope andBaba Gentle(played byRichard Moll), as he's convinced that the cult really loves him, as Mork, posing as "Baba Loo", and Mindy both infiltrate the cult to try to retrive him, as Baba Hope orders Baba Gentle, who acts as the cult's "enforcer", to seize them.

characters.name: Amy Babish
characters.description: Amy BabishLinda Gillen as Amy Babish Mrs. Babish's daughter, in "The Slow Child" in Season 3 of "Laverne & Shirley" (ep.#15).Biographical informationGenderFemaleFamily:Edna Babish(mother)Frank DeFazio(stepfather)Laverne DeFazio(stepsister)About:Mentally challenged daughter of Mrs. Babish, who at first objects to her being treated as "one of the girls" by Laverne and Shirley; begins datingLennyas the two both take a shine to one anotherBehind the scenesActorLinda GillenAppeared onLaverne & ShirleyEpisodes appeared in:"The Slow Child" in Season 3 Amy Babishwas a character onLaverne & Shirleywho appeared in theSeason 3episode (#15) "The Slow Child". The part of Amy Babish was played in the episode byLinda Gillen, who also appears asLindain theSeason 4episode "A Date with Eraserhead" (episode #7). In the episode "The Slow Child" in Season 3 (episode #15) its revealed thatEdna Babish, has a mentally challenged daughter named Amy. Edna doesn't particularly care for the way thatLaverneandShirleytreat Amy as "one of the girls", especially when Amy andLennybegin dating, but in the end, she realizes she has been treating her daughter as if she is a small child and not allowing her to grow.

characters.name: Bebo
characters.description: BeboBiographical informationGenderUnknownFamily:????About:Mork's pet from Ork who zips around the floor at quick pace; Mork is unsure if Bebo is male or femaleBehind the scenesActorHimselfAppeared onMork and MindyEpisodes appeared in:21 episodes inSeason 3 Bebois Mork's pet naugachomp, who is seen throughoutseason 3ofMork & Mindy. A small mound of fur, Bebo zips around the floor at a quick pace. Always referred to as "him," Mork once commented that he's unsure whether Bebo is male or female because he never had the nerve to turn him over. A small mound of fur, Bebo zips around the floor at a quick pace. Always referred to as "him," Mork once commented that he's unsure whether Bebo is male or female because he never had the nerve to turn him over. Bebo is sometimes seen in Mork & Mindy's apartment, but most often he appears next to Mork during his reports to Orson.

characters.name: Belle
characters.description: BelleisK.C. Cunningham's mother andDick Cunningham's wife. She is played bySue Anne Langdonin the Season 10 episode, "All I Want for Christmas". Like K.C., she speaks with a hick accent.

characters.name: Mr. Bickley
characters.description: Mr. BickleyTom Poston as Mr. BickleyBiographical informationGenderMaleJob/Career:Landlord / Greeting Card WriterAbout:Rather grouchy, irasible type who eventually warms up to Mindy and Mork; is never able to find out about Mork being an ailen, although he's seen the the enormous nest for Mork's egg in the McConnells' apartmentBehind the scenesActorTom PostonAppeared onMork and MindyEpisodes appeared in:54 in series Franklin "Frank" Delano Bickleywas a main and recurring character who appeared in 54 of the 95 episodes ofMork and Mindy. The part of Mr. Bickley was played on the series byTom Poston. Mr. Bickley isMorkandMindy McConnell's downstairs neighbor. In his first appearance, he does not like Mork or Mindy, but soon warms up to them after Mork gets him a dog, which he namesBickey, after Mork gives Franklin this nickname. He slowly begins to open up to his neighbors and eventually comes to call them his best friends, even if they occasionally get on his nerves.

He slowly begins to open up to his neighbors and eventually comes to call them his best friends, even if they occasionally get on his nerves.

characters.name: Tom Bickley
characters.description: Tom BickleyBiographical informationGenderMaleBehind the scenesActorTom SullivanAppeared onMork & Mindy Tom Bickleyis the adult son ofMr. Bickley. Tom, who makes his living as a singer, is blind and believes his father has rejected him because of his handicap. He is portrayed by Tom Sullivan, and appears in the Season 2 episodeMork Learns to See. Tom lives life to the fullest—he mentions waterskiing, jogging and skydiving as some of his leisure activities—but he wishes his father would accept him for who he is rather than being ashamed of him.
characters.personality_section: Tom lives life to the fullest—he mentions waterskiing, jogging and skydiving as some of his leisure activities—but he wishes his father would accept him for who he is rather than being ashamed of him.

characters.name: Big Rosie Greenbaum
characters.description: Big Rosie Greenbaumis a recurring character onLaverne & Shirley. She is portrayed byCarole Ita White.

characters.name: Billy
characters.description: BillyCorey Feldman as Billy in the Season 3 episode "Mork the Prankster" (ep.#6).Biographical informationGenderMaleAbout:Child who attends the daycare center where Mork worksBehind the scenesActorCorey FeldmanAppeared onMork and MindyEpisodes appeared in:2 inSeason 3First appeared in:"Mork the Prankster"(Season 3 premiere)Last appeared in:"Gunfight at the Mork-Kay Corral"(Season 3) Billyis one of the children who attends the daycare center where Mork works who appears in two episodes ofMork and Mindyin Season 3. The part of Billy is played on the series byCorey Feldman. When Mork and Billy and the other kids at the day care center decide to dress up costume as their favorite characters (Stephanie comes as Sara Lee, Lola as Truman Capote). Billy's father wants him to follow in his footsteps as a doctor, but the boy relates to someone with his own name, Billy the Kid. When Mork and Billy and the other kids at the day care center decide to dress up costume as their favorite characters (Stephanie comes as Sara Lee, Lola as Truman Capote). Billy's father wants him to follow in his footsteps as a doctor, but the boy relates to someone with his own name, Billy the Kid. Decked out in Western attire, Billy challenges Mork to a "shootout" with water balloons in a nearby ghost town, clearly unaware of the murderous nature of the real William H. Bonney. In trying to break up the "showdown", Mindy ends up all wet from a water balloon, as Billy runs away to fight another day.

characters.name: Lt. Colonel Binicky
characters.description: Lt. Colonel BinickyDave Ketchum as Lt. Col. Binicky in "R.O.T.C." in Season 2 ofHappy DaysBiographical informationGenderMaleJob/Career:ROTC instructor atJefferson High School, retired U.S. Army officer (0-5, Retired)About:Gives Richie, who he made new ROTC squad leader, bad advice on leading his squadBehind the scenesActorDave KetchumAppeared onHappy DaysEpisodes appeared in:"R.O.T.C."(Season 2) Lt. Colonel Bickleyis a character who appears in theSeason 2episode ofHappy Daystitled"R.O.T.C."(episode #5). The part of Lt. Col. Binicky is played in the episode byDave Ketchum, who also appears asCoach Pelinoin theSeason 4episode"A Shot in the Dark"(episode #15), and also makes a guest appearance onMork & Mindyas a salesman in the Season 1 episode"Mork's First Christmas"(episode #12). When Richie tries to go to Colonel Binicky, who has just made him the new leader of his ROTC squad, to tell him about his squad running off on him, Binicky's response is, "Good!" "The day you learn the secet of good leadership...the men hate your guts!"

characters.name: Binky Hodges
characters.description: Binky Hodgesis a fictional character who appears in the episodeBe My ValentineonHappy Days. He was played by actorChristopher Knightof "The Brady Bunch." Binky is a student atJefferson High SchoolwithJoanie Cunningham, who he later dates. Joanie has a dream about him while waiting for him to get off work for their date. He ends up becoming her first kiss.

characters.description: Binky Hodgesis a fictional character who appears in the episodeBe My ValentineonHappy Days. He was played by actorChristopher Knightof "The Brady Bunch." Binky is a student atJefferson High SchoolwithJoanie Cunningham, who he later dates. Joanie has a dream about him while waiting for him to get off work for their date. He ends up becoming her first kiss.

characters.name: Nancy Blansky
characters.description: Nancy BlanskyNancy Walker as Nancy Blansky in "Blansky's Beauties".Biographical informationFamily:Howard Cunningham(cousin)Marion Cunningham(cousin-in-law)About:plays den mother to a group of Las Vegas Showgirls whom she also manages, as they reside in the apartment complex she ownsBehind the scenesActorNancy WalkerAppeared onHappy Days(guest appearance)Blansky's BeautiesEpisodes appeared in:"The Third Anniversary Show(Season 3,Happy Days)13 episodes,Blansky's Beauties Nancy Blanskywas a character who appeared in theSeason 3episode ofHappy Daystitled "The Third Anniversary Show" (episode #17), which aired on ABC-TV on February 4, 1977. The part of Nancy is played in the episode byNancy Walker, who made a guest appearance, as she would reprise the role of Nancy the very next week in a spinoff seriesBlansky's Beauties, which premiered on ABC-TV on February 12, 1977. When Richie and Joanie decide to throw a surprise wedding anniversary party for their parents, included in the surprise is an unexpected visit fromHoward'scousin Nancy, who owns an apartment complex in Las Vegas.

characters.name: Burglar in "The Cunningham Caper"
characters.description: Burglar in "The Cunningham Caper"Herb Edelman as "The Burglar" in "The Cunningham Caper" in Season 2 (ep.#14).Biographical informationAbout:His attempt to burglarize the Cunningham home fails miserably, as Fonzie, in delivering a pizza to the home, locks the burglar in the closet with a chair until the cops came to pick him upBehind the scenesActorHerb EdelmanAppeared onHappy DaysEpisodes appeared in:"The Cunningham Caper"(Season 2) The Burglarwas a character who appeared inSeason 2episode ofHappy Daystitled"The Cunningham Caper"(ep.#14). The part of the burglar is played in the episode byHerb Edelman. When Richie, who's battling the flu, Ralph and Potsie become locked in the bathroom while they decide on a way to escape from the burglar, who broke into the Cunningham house while Howard, Marion and Joanie were away at the movie theatre, Ralph goes through a small window, but is caught when he doesn't jump out of a tree.

characters.name: Captain Nirvana of the Necrotons
characters.description: Captain Nirvana of the NecrotonsRaquel Welch as Captain NirvanaBiographical informationGenderFemaleBehind the scenesActorRaquel WelchEpisodes appeared in:Mork vs. The Necrotons, Part 1Mork vs. The Necrotons, Part 2 Captain Nirvana is the Captain of theNecrotons, a race of sultry women who have come to invade and destroy Earth. At first she wants to interrogateMorkunder torture, then attempts to suck out his brain, but she eventually develops a crush on him. In the end she allows Mork to run away because she realizes that is what a friend would do. Captain Nirvana is portrayed byRaquel Welch.

characters.name: Charlene
characters.description: Charleneis a girl who appeared inThe Fonz is Allergic to Girls. She is played by Rebecca Gilchrist. Charlene has red, curly hair that she wears up and fair skin. LikeDoris, Charlene seems to like romance, but only expresses interest inFonzie, despitePotsie WeberandRalph Malphexpressing interest in her. She also seems to have a playful side, as she told a joke. characters.name: Chuck Cunningham
characters.actor_name: Ric Carrott("Love and the Happy Days")Gavan O'Herlihy(Season 1)Randolph Roberts(Season 2)
characters.description: Chuck CunninghamO'HerlihyRobertsCarrottBiographical informationGenderMaleFamily:Howard Cunningham (father)Marion Cunningham (mother)Richie Cunningham (little brother)Joanie Cunningham (little sister)Behind the scenesActorRic Carrott("Love and the Happy Days")Gavan O'Herlihy(Season 1)Randolph Roberts(Season 2)Appeared onHappy Days Charles "Chuck" Cunninghamis the oldest son ofHoward CunninghamandMarion Cunninghamand the older brother ofRichie CunninghamandJoanie Cunningham. He was played by three different actors. In the "Love and the Happy Days" pilot, he was played byRic Carrott, but in the first two seasons ofHappy Days, he was played byGavan O'Herlihy, who was replaced byRandolph Robertsfor two episodes in the second season. Chuck Cunningham was a high school jock atJefferson High, who was known for making rare appearances at home. Almost always seen with a basketball, he shared an upstairs room with Richie until he went away to college, possibly on a basketball scholarship. Richie briefly stays at his place for a time. He had a big appetite and was often eating when he wasn't shooting baskets off the garage. One Christmas, he recommends to his father to getFonzieto fix the family Santa Claus display. Chuck also tends to suck his thumb when he's worried, a trait he shares with his sister,Joanie Cunningham.

characters.name: Clarabelle the Clown
characters.description: Clarabelle the ClownBuffalo Bob Smith and Clarabelle the Clown visit the Cunninghams in"The Howdy Doody Show"in Season 2 ofHappy Days(ep.#17).Biographical informationGenderMaleAbout:Mute partner ofBuffalo Bob SmithonThe Howdy Doody Show; Richie reluctantly chooses to destroy photo film after Buffalo Bob tells him to keep the actor's actual face concealed from the public, after sneaking backstage and taking snapshots of him without his makeup on after a TV show performanceBehind the scenesActorBob BrunnerAppeared onHappy DaysEpisodes appeared in:The Howdy Doody ShowClarabell the Clownwas the mute partner ofHowdy Doodyon the highly popular 1950's children's TV seriesThe Howdy Doody Show; onHappy Daysthe part of Clarabell is played by producer/writerBob Brunnerin theSeason 2episode"The Howdy Doody Show"; formerHowdy Doody ShowhostBuffalo Bob Smithalso appears in the episode. Three actors played Clarabell on a regular basis. The first wasBob Keeshan, who later becameCaptain Kangaroo. Keeshan was succeeded by Robert"Nick"Nicholson,[1]who also played the character of J. Cornelius Cobb onThe Howdy Doody Show.Lew Anderson[2]was the third and last person to play Clarabell. Anderson played the character from 1954 until the series' final episode on September 24, 1960.  Anderson returned to play Clarabell in the short-lived 1976–77New Howdy Doody Showand in the 1987 40th anniversary special, and in later years in many personal appearances with Buffalo Bob Smith. In addition, Dayton Allen, Bill LeCornec, and others played Clarabell in the early years if Keeshan was busy doing something else for the show. Clarabell, who wore a baggy, striped costume, communicated through mime and by honking a horn for"YES"or"NO". Clarabell would also spray fellow cast memberBuffalo Bob Smithwithseltzer.

characters.name: Glenda Faye Comstock
characters.description: Glenda Faye ComstockCrissy Wilzak as Glenda Faye Comstock on "Mork & Mindy".Biographical informationGenderFemaleDomestic partner(s)/Fiancee:Nelson Flavor(dated)Behind the scenesActorCrissy WilzakAppeared onMork and MindyEpisodes appeared in:23 in Season 3 Glenda Faye ComstockisMindy's close friend. She first appears in 23 episodes inSeason 3ofMork & Mindy. The part of Glenda Faye is portrayed byCrissy Wilzak. Mindy's friend Glenda, who's a recent widow after the death of her husband, became the love interest of Mindy's politician cousinNelson Flavor(Jim Staahl) and was only seen in Season 3.

characters.name: Cook
characters.description: Rick Hurstas Cook Cookis a good friend ofRalph MalphandPat, and member ofThe Gems. He only appeared in "All the Way", and was most likely written out of the series. Because Cook had very little screentime, not much is known about him. We do know that he likes girls and Hot Rods. Because Cook had very little screentime, not much is known about him. We do know that he likes girls and Hot Rods.

characters.actor_name: Harold Gould("Love and the Television Set")Tom Bosley(Happy Days)
characters.description: Howard CunninghamTom Bosley as "Mr. C"Biographical informationGenderMaleJob/Career:Owner of Cunningham hardware, Milwaukee, WI, U.S.Family:Mother Kelp(mother-in-law)Unnamed motherSean "Cap" Cunningham (father)Dick Cunningham(brother)Richie Cunningham(son)Joanie Cunningham(daughter)Chuck Cunningham(son)Roger Phillips(nephew)Flip Phillips (cousin-in-law)K.C. Cunningham(niece)Charles "Chachi" Arcola(son in-law)Lori Beth Allen-Cunningham(daughter in-law)Spouse:Marion Cunningham(wife)About:Understanding father, who wants to support his kids in any way to make them happy, but also teach them the consequences in life actions as wellBehind the scenesActorHarold Gould("Love and the Television Set")Tom Bosley(Happy Days)Appeared onLove American Style/Happy DaysEpisodes appeared in:255 in seriesFirst appeared in:"Love and the Happy Days" (Love American Style)Last appeared in:"Passages: Part 1" & "Passages: Part 2" Howard C. Cunninghamwas a main character on the ABC-TV sitcomHappy Days. He was played byTom Bosleyfor the full 11 seasons ofHappy Days. He was also played byHarold Gouldin the episode ofLove American Styletitled "Love and the Happy Days", which inspiredHappy DaysproducerGarry Marshallto create theHappy DaysTV series. Often called "Mr. C." By Fonzie,Ralph Malph, andWarren "Potsie" Weber, is the Father ofCharles, or "Chuck",RichieandJoanie Cunninghamand the husband ofMarion Cunningham. Mr. C owned his own hardware store,Cunningham Hardware, as a profession.

characters.name: Joanie Cunningham
characters.actor_name: Susan Neher("Love and the Television Set")Audrey Pfeifer (Happy Days1973 Pilot)Erin Moran(Happy Days)
characters.description: Joanie CunninghamErin MoranSusan NeherBiographical informationAKA/Nickame(s):"Shortcake" (by Fonzie)"Joanes" (by Jenny)GenderFemaleBirthnameJoanie Louise CunninghamJob/Career:Teacher atJefferson High School, former ticket girl at the movie houseFamily:Marion Cunningham(mother)Howard Cunningham(father)Richie Cunningham(brother)Chuck Cunningham(brother)Lori Beth Allen-Cunningham(sister-in-law)Richie Cunningham, Jr.(nephew)Roger Phillips(cousin)Flip Phillips(cousin)KC Cunningham(cousin)Sean Cunningham(grandfather)Spouse:Chachi ArcolaAbout:Takes a teaching job at the high school with Fonzie and Roger as faculty membersBehind the scenesActorSusan Neher("Love and the Television Set")Audrey Pfeifer (Happy Days1973 Pilot)Erin Moran(Happy Days)Appeared onHappy Days/Joanie Loves ChachiEpisodes appeared in:239 onHappy Days/ 17 onJoanie Loves Chachi Joanie Louise Cunningham-Arcolais a character inHappy Days.She was originally a minor character in the earlier seasons of the series, but she gradually became one of the regulars. She was the only daughter and youngest child ofHowardandMarion Cunningham, and the younger sister ofChuckandRichie Cunningham. She became part of Fonzie's surrogate family when he moved into the room over Cunningham's garage in Season 3; he took to affectionately calling her "Shortcake". Joanie was the quintessential adolescent girl from the 1950s; in the earlier seasons she was more of a typical annoying "kid sister" type, a "mole" who was always wanting to peek in on Richie's makeout sessions, or get wind of any juicy gossip. One of the earlier recurring gags in the series involved Joanie, in which she was frequently ordered (usually by Howard or Marion, but also occasionally by Richie) to "Go to your room", either as punishment for her attitude, or more often to keep her from hearing things not meant for her younger ears. Joanie also had a very sassy personality and was quick to cast off barbs at others, even her own parents. One example of this was when Marion was fitting Howard for a costume of Julius Caesar; when he asked Joanie what she thought of his outfit she quipped, "No wonder they stabbed him", which earned the deadpan observation from Howard that, "She's getting such a fresh mouth, Marion".

characters.name: K.C. Cunningham
characters.description: K.C. CunninghamBiographical informationGenderFemaleFamily:Belle(mother)Dick Cunningham(father)Howard Cunningham(uncle)Marion Cunningham(aunt in-law)Richie Cunningham(cousin)Joanie Cunningham(cousin)Chuck Cunningham(cousin)About:Moved in with the Cunninghams after daughterJoaniehad left withChachifor ChicagoLeft an all-girls boarding school in Texas for Milwaukee when it closed downQuickly becomes friends withJenny Piccalo, and then begins dating for the first timeBehind the scenesActorCrystal BernardAppeared onHappy DaysEpisodes appeared in:15 episodes in Season 10First appeared in:"A Night at the Circus"Last appeared in:"Affairs of the Heart" K.C. Cunninghamis a character who appears inSeason 10ofHappy Days. The part of K.C. is played byCrystal Bernard. K.C. Cunningham,Howard'sniece, moved in with him and Marion after daughterJoanieleft withChachifor Chicago. K.C. had just left an all-girls boarding school in Texas because it closed down. Her parents are always traveling. She also became friends withJenny Piccalo(and later,Flip Phillips), and she went on her first date withMelvin Belvin. K.C. Cunningham is a lively and friendly hick girl, and would hang out with her friends (most notably Jenny Piccalo). She is shown to care for her friends and family, with examples being:
